disease_info.description: Bacterial skin infections occur when Staphylococcus or Streptococcus species invade the skin barrier. They often present with redness, warmth, swelling, pus formation, or painful lesions.
disease_info.common_symptoms: Redness, swelling, warmth, pus, tenderness, fever (in severe cases)
disease_info.recommended_action: Seek medical care; antibiotics may be required. Clean wound surfaces and avoid scratching.
disease_info.image_indicators: Pus-filled lesions, yellow crusts, spreading redness, inflamed patches
